name: DeepSeek-V3.1 (Thinking)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (671B total / 37B active) — DeepSeekMoE + Multi-head Latent Attention, hybrid think/non-think chat template
note: Thinking mode of the hybrid V3.1 checkpoint; served via deepseek-reasoner, faster than R1-0528 at similar quality
tpsNote: Exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at Q4 (~405GB); requires multi-GPU or heavy CPU offload

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, text-only subset, Pass@1
BrowseComp (Acc.), Thinking mode, from official HF model card benchmark table
Codeforces-Div1 Rating, Thinking mode, from official HF model card benchmark table
Aider-Polyglot (Acc.), Thinking mode, from official HF model card benchmark table

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via HF model card + DeepSeek API docs release post (2025-08-21). Same architecture facts as non-thinking variant confirmed. All 6 thinking-mode benchmark rows confirmed verbatim on the HF model card. Deprecated status and pricing confirmed the same way as the non-thinking record.

2026-07-16: Checked all 5 flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, humaneval, swe-bench, mmmu) against (1) the official HF model card benchmark table (huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1) and DeepSeek's own release post (api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news250821/), (2) Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/deepseek-v3-1-reasoning) and Epoch AI (epoch.ai/models/deepseek-v3-1), and (3) the sibling deepseek-v3-1-non-thinking.json and deepseek-r1-0528.json records already in this corpus. Findings: raw MMLU, MATH, and HumanEval do not appear anywhere in the official table for either Thinking or Non-Thinking config — DeepSeek replaced them with MMLU-Redux/MMLU-Pro and AIME/HMMT/LiveCodeBench respectively, the same substitution already visible in this family's R1-0528 update (which likewise dropped raw mmlu/math that R1-671B had reported), so this reads as a deliberate reporting-practice shift, not an oversight. SWE-bench Verified (Agent mode) is reported only in the Non-Thinking column of the official table (66.0, already captured on deepseek-v3-1-non-thinking.json) and is explicitly left blank ("—") in the Thinking column, i.e. DeepSeek itself did not run/publish that eval for this exact config. MMMU is absent from the official table entirely and this checkpoint is vision:false/text-only, so a multimodal benchmark would not be expected; Artificial Analysis only names "MMMU-Pro" as an evaluation category with no numeric score surfaced for this model, and Epoch AI's page states outright "No coding benchmarks evaluated," "No math and science benchmarks evaluated," and "No multimodal benchmarks evaluated" for DeepSeek-V3.1, scoring it only on WeirdML/Fiction.liveBench/SimpleBench/ForecastBench. vals.ai has no MMLU benchmark page at all and its SWE-bench Verified leaderboard does not list any DeepSeek-V3.1 variant. Note: DeepSeek-V3.1-Terminus (a distinct, later Sept-2025 checkpoint update, not this Aug-21 V3.1 release) reportedly scores ~72-74 on SWE-bench Verified per third-party writeups, but that number belongs to a different model file and was deliberately not carried over here to avoid conflating checkpoints. All 5 gaps are treated as genuine, deliberate publication absences and left omitted rather than estimated.

2026-07-18: Re-chased the swe-bench and mmmu gaps flagged for this record with a fresh, deeper pass across all 6 recommended methods, deliberately trying to falsify the prior 2026-07-16 conclusion rather than just repeat it.

SWE-BENCH VERIFIED: Re-fetched the official HF model card table (huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1) directly (not from memory) and confirmed again, cell-by-cell: "SWE Verified (Agent mode)" = 66.0 in the Non-Thinking column, "—" (explicitly blank) in the Thinking column; same blank pattern for "SWE-bench Multilingual (Agent mode)" (54.5 Non-Thinking, "—" Thinking). Checked the original DeepSeek release post (api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news250821/) — its benchmark numbers are embedded as images (v3.1_benchmark_*.webp) not extractable text, but the surrounding copy only claims "Better results on SWE / Terminal-Bench" in aggregate, no separate Thinking-mode figure in prose. Checked independent trackers: Epoch AI (epoch.ai/models/deepseek-v3-1) shows no SWE-bench score and ranks the model only on its general Capabilities Index; Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/deepseek-v3-1-reasoning) lists no SWE-bench Verified score for the reasoning/thinking config; BenchLM (benchlm.ai/models/deepseek-v3-1) explicitly has no SWE-bench row populated ("missing categories stay blank until a sourced evaluation is available"); vals.ai's SWE-bench Verified leaderboard page does not surface a DeepSeek-V3.1 entry in the visible top results; llm-stats.com's "deepseek-reasoner" page (the API alias for DeepSeek's Thinking-mode endpoint) now resolves to the current-generation DeepSeek-V3.2 (Thinking) model rather than the Aug-2025 V3.1 checkpoint, so it cannot supply a historical V3.1-Thinking number. Checked academic/sibling sources: the DeepSeek-V3.2 arXiv paper (arxiv.org/abs/2512.02556, HTML version arxiv.org/html/2512.02556v1) and the Kimi K2 technical report (arxiv.org/pdf/2507.20534) were both searched for any table citing DeepSeek-V3.1-Thinking's SWE-bench score as a baseline; neither contains one (DeepSeek-V3.2's paper only compares against V3.1-Terminus, a later Sept-2025 checkpoint, and Kimi K2's report doesn't include DeepSeek-V3.1 in its SWE-bench table at all in the accessible text). Note on a red herring: several web-search AI summaries (not primary sources) casually label the 66.0 Non-Thinking Agent-mode score as "DeepSeek-V3.1-Thinking" or "DeepSeek-V3.1-Think" — this is very likely careless conflation of the two config columns by secondary blogs/aggregators, not a genuine distinct Thinking-mode measurement, since the one primary source that actually breaks out the two columns (the official HF card) explicitly leaves Thinking blank in both fetches performed this pass. Given that ambiguity and the already-correct attribution of 66.0 to deepseek-v3-1-non-thinking.json, this pass does not carry that number over to the Thinking record. Conclusion unchanged from 2026-07-16: DeepSeek did not publish/run a SWE-bench Verified number for the Thinking configuration specifically; left omitted rather than estimated or borrowed from the sibling config.

MMMU: Re-confirmed via corpus fields that this checkpoint is modalities:["text"] / capabilities.vision:false — i.e. text-only, so MMMU (an image+text benchmark) is architecturally inapplicable, not merely unpublished. Also re-confirmed no vendor publishes a number for it anyway: absent from the official HF benchmark table entirely (re-fetched, confirmed no MMMU row), absent from BenchLM's tracked-benchmark list for this model, and Artificial Analysis only lists "MMMU-Pro" as an evaluation-category label with no numeric score surfaced for this specific model/config. Epoch AI's page corroborates with an explicit "No multimodal benchmarks evaluated" statement for DeepSeek-V3.1. Both the architectural-inapplicability check and the exhaustive-search check agree: omit.
